jokes.text: You can do some serious subway flirting before you realize the guy is homeless.
jokes.setup: Complaining about January when everyone wears bulky coats
jokes.punchline: The reveal that coat-obscured attractiveness leads to flirting with homeless people
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of typical winter complaint into specific, relatable urban dating mishap. Perfect economy and unexpected turn.

jokes.text: Oh, is that where the word 'sad' comes from?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions 'seasonal affective disorder'
jokes.punchline: Liz thinks 'sad' is an acronym derived from the psychological term
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd linguistic confusion that perfectly captures Liz's character while being structurally elegant. Shows her overthinking simple things.

jokes.text: I've been stuck inside playing online boggle. It's messing with my head. Star, rats, arts, tars.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining why she's confused about 'sad'
jokes.punchline: She demonstrates her mental state by listing anagram variations
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Liz's nerdy obsessions affecting her thinking. Well-integrated but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: How is female Larry Bird holding hands with a guy? What am I doing wrong?
jokes.setup: Looking at photo of unattractive woman with boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Liz's self-deprecating confusion about her own romantic failures
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz self-deprecation with sports metaphor. Solid character work but predictable setup.

jokes.text: I'm telling Nancy that I'm with female Kevin McHale.
jokes.setup: Jack photographing Liz after she complained about female Larry Bird photo
jokes.punchline: Jack continues the unflattering basketball player comparison by calling Liz female Kevin McHale
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that extends the sports metaphor. Good economy but fairly straightforward follow-up.

jokes.text: I have elaborate fantasies of her husband dying in a boat explosion.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about Jack's relationship with Nancy being 'just friends'
jokes.punchline: Jack's casually delivered violent fantasy about Nancy's husband
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy line - sophisticated delivery of completely inappropriate thought. Dark humor with excellent character integration and specificity of 'boat explosion.'

jokes.text: Stop. Pots. Tops. Opts. Post.
jokes.setup: Liz leaving elevator
jokes.punchline: She continues the boggle-obsessed anagram behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that maintains character consistency. Not particularly funny but good continuity.

jokes.text: Lutz, do the right thing. / It's Lutz's turn to pick where we eat today. / Oh, boy. Lutz, don't do this.
jokes.setup: Building tension about Lutz picking restaurant
jokes.punchline: The dramatic pleading reveals how terrible Lutz's choices are
jokes.explanation: Good escalating tension around mundane office decision. Sets up Lutz as terrible choice-maker.

jokes.text: Subway. / I hope you die!
jokes.setup: All the dramatic pleading about Lutz's restaurant choice
jokes.punchline: He picks Subway, someone wishes death on him
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation payoff. The extreme overreaction to choosing Subway is brilliantly disproportionate and economical.

jokes.text: Maybe some people won't be coming back next year.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to calm everyone down about getting through the season
jokes.punchline: Casually threatening job security in the name of morale
jokes.explanation: Dark meta-humor about show business uncertainty. Liz's terrible management style on full display.

jokes.text: Jonathan, why do I have an Indian assistant if my computer is always... Jack. I'm messing with you.
jokes.setup: Jack appears to be making racist complaint about assistant
jokes.punchline: Nancy reveals she's deliberately messing with the video call quality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts expectation about Jack being genuinely offensive. Nancy gets the upper hand briefly.

jokes.text: I'm tired of waking up to a porch full of dead squirrels.
jokes.setup: Nancy explaining she needs to scrape icicles off gutters
jokes.punchline: The bizarre specific detail about dead squirrels
jokes.explanation: Vivid, weird detail that paints picture of Nancy's grim winter reality. Good specificity and unexpectedness.

jokes.text: I'm messing with you... Oh, very funny, Donna
jokes.setup: Jack thinks there are real technical difficulties
jokes.punchline: Nancy reveals she was pranking him, then the computer actually freezes
jokes.explanation: Nice double misdirection - first the fake technical problems, then real ones. Shows their playful relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: He was pretty bad. Nutmeg. Rodeo, rodeo, rodeo.
jokes.setup: Discussion of failed R-rated hypnotist show
jokes.punchline: Pete apparently still under hypnotic suggestion saying random words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant throwaway gag that Pete is still hypnotized. Random word choices are perfectly absurd. Great economy.

jokes.text: I can rent a convertible, You can complain about the heat with elderly people.
jokes.setup: Pete selling Liz on Miami trip
jokes.punchline: He knows exactly what would appeal to Liz - complaining with old people
jokes.explanation: Perfect understanding of Liz's personality - she would enjoy complaining about weather with elderly people. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Cross promotional, Deal mechanics, Revenue streams, Jargon, Synergy.
jokes.setup: Pete making presentation to Jack
jokes.punchline: His entire presentation is just business buzzwords including 'Jargon'
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on corporate speak that literally includes the word 'jargon' as jargon. Brilliant economy and self-awareness.

jokes.text: That's the best presentation I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Pete's presentation was literally just buzzwords
jokes.punchline: Jack is genuinely impressed by the meaningless corporate speak
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - he's so corporate that pure jargon impresses him. Good ironic payoff.

jokes.text: But I... I bought a parrot shirt.
jokes.setup: Jack changing destination from Miami to Boston
jokes.punchline: Pete's defeated confession about his premature tropical shirt purchase
jokes.explanation: Sad, specific detail about Pete's optimism being crushed. 'Parrot shirt' is a great specific choice.

jokes.text: It is a city with an NBA team. And even though Will Smith never wrote any raps about it, the poet Robert Lowell lived there.
jokes.setup: Trying to make Boston sound appealing
jokes.punchline: Contrasting Will Smith's 'Miami' with obscure poet Robert Lowell as selling points
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between pop culture (Will Smith's Miami song) and high culture (Robert Lowell). Perfect Liz trying too hard to be intellectual.

jokes.text: Well, not in Boston, but nearby. No, not tufts... Shut up. / Harvard.
jokes.setup: Writer claiming he went to college in Boston area
jokes.punchline: The progression from defensive to being told to shut up to finally admitting Harvard
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing writer's false modesty about Harvard. The 'not Tufts' detail is nice specificity.

jokes.text: Also, there is no Spectravision or Internet in the hotel, So plan ahead, gentlemen, porn-wise.
jokes.setup: Pete listing hotel limitations
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact advice about planning for pornography needs
jokes.explanation: Appropriately awkward manager moment. 'Porn-wise' is a great awkward construction.

jokes.text: You think I like sharing a room with Toofer? Dude is still playing that Obama song.
jokes.setup: Frank complaining about rooming arrangements
jokes.punchline: Specific complaint about Toofer's continued Obama celebration music
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific complaint. References the time period and Toofer's political enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Maybe because one of us still hasn't read the other's screenplay. / I read it. I just didn't like it.
jokes.setup: Frank's passive-aggressive comment about unread screenplay
jokes.punchline: Lutz's brutal honesty that he read it but hated it
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where the truth is worse than being ignored. Lutz's blunt honesty is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Usually designers just make clothes for me, But when I do buy stuff, I'm a child's medium.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking Cerie's clothing size for sharing
jokes.punchline: Cerie's casual mention of having clothes designed for her, then wearing children's sizes
jokes.explanation: Good Cerie character moment showing her oblivious privilege and tiny size. The designer clothes detail is perfectly her.

jokes.text: These are Jamie Foxx for assfarm.
jokes.setup: Jenna claiming she only wears designer labels
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous fake designer name 'Jamie Foxx for assfarm'
jokes.explanation: Absurd fake fashion label that sounds plausible until you process 'assfarm.' Good Jenna delusion.

jokes.text: I was making my thing up. You bitch! / What did you tell her, you vondruke?
jokes.setup: Cerie revealing she made up gossip about Sue
jokes.punchline: The escalation to 'bitch' then Jenna's invented German insult 'vondruke'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Jenna's made-up German accent and insult. 'Vondruke' is a funny nonsense word.

jokes.text: Thank you, thank you. It's great to be here, Cleveland. / You suck. / I suck? The rock and roll hall of fame sucks!
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he gets in trouble on the road
jokes.punchline: Flashback showing him insulting Cleveland and escalating the fight
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy character moment showing his inability to handle hecklers. Cleveland-specific insult works well.

jokes.text: What did you tell her, you vondruke?
jokes.setup: Sue entering the argument with her German accent
jokes.punchline: Her made-up German insult
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. 'Vondruke' sounds appropriately fake-German and insulting.

jokes.text: These gentlemen are the writers for Bruins Beat, Whose offices we'll be sharing. They are all named Sean. They are mean, and I hate it here.
jokes.setup: Kenneth introducing the Boston writers
jokes.punchline: They're all named Sean and he immediately declares he hates it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Boston stereotype of everyone named Sean. Kenneth's immediate hatred is uncharacteristically direct and funny.

jokes.text: Seven items are different. See if you can spot which ones.
jokes.setup: Liz complaining the office replication isn't identical
jokes.punchline: Jack turning it into a Highlights magazine spot-the-difference game
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to children's puzzle magazines. Jack's casual attitude about the office differences is perfectly him.

jokes.text: Oh, that'll really disappoint your key demographic of drunken 11-year-olds.
jokes.setup: Liz saying the show might not happen
jokes.punchline: Jack's assessment of their audience as drunk children
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cutting assessment of late-night TV audience. Perfect Jack contempt delivered with precision.

jokes.text: What keeps people polite on airplanes? A shared hatred of the CBS sitcoms They're forced to watch.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the concept of a common enemy
jokes.punchline: Airline passengers united by hatred of CBS programming
jokes.explanation: Smart media industry joke that works on multiple levels. Good specific reference to airline entertainment.

jokes.text: I find that news anchors and breasts are always smaller than they look on TV.
jokes.setup: Nancy commenting that the news set is small
jokes.punchline: Jack's comparison between news anchors and breasts both appearing smaller in person
jokes.explanation: Clever parallel structure joke that's appropriately inappropriate for Jack. Good economy and delivery.

jokes.text: Tonight's top story, A Worcester man accused of trading his foster son for gasoline. And later in the hour, Ten tips on how to make your cat's birthday go purr-fectly.
jokes.setup: Nancy trying out being a news anchor
jokes.punchline: The jarring contrast between dark child abuse story and cute cat birthday tips
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy juxtaposition typical of local news. The pun 'purr-fectly' makes the contrast even starker.

jokes.text: Apparently Brad Pitt and Sharon Stone did a crap job rebuilding those Katrina homes, and Mark thinks he can make a fortune down there.
jokes.setup: Nancy explaining why her husband left for New Orleans
jokes.punchline: Blaming celebrities for bad construction work in a disaster zone
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference to celebrity charity work gone wrong. Realistic cynicism about disaster capitalism.

jokes.text: neither one of us know what to do about the dog, 'cause we both hate it.
jokes.setup: Nancy listing their marital problems
jokes.punchline: The casual admission that they both hate their dog
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. The mutual pet hatred is a perfect marital dysfunction detail.

jokes.text: Yeah, we talked to Dr. Phil, and then we made a collage in our dream journals.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if they've talked openly about divorce
jokes.punchline: Nancy's sarcastic description of new-age therapy nonsense instead of real communication
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pop psychology solutions. The specificity of 'collage in dream journals' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: They just run off to New Orleans And jump on that B.S. Saints bandwagon. Go pats.
jokes.setup: Nancy explaining that people in Boston don't talk about problems
jokes.punchline: Connecting it to sports team bandwagon-jumping and showing her Patriots loyalty
jokes.explanation: Good regional character detail. The Patriots loyalty is authentic Boston behavior.

jokes.text: I do if you can resuscitate an electrocuted person.
jokes.setup: Nancy asking if he can rewire a doorbell
jokes.punchline: Jack's conditional yes based on being able to revive electrocution victims
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that acknowledges Jack's overconfidence and incompetence with practical tasks. Good economy.

jokes.text: Can you wire the doorbell to the dog?
jokes.setup: Nancy asking for help with her house problems
jokes.punchline: Suggesting electrocuting the dog they both hate
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the dog hatred with dark solution. Extremely economical and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: You lying, white devil! The only people you set free were rich, white dudes like yourself!
jokes.setup: Historical reenactor playing John Hancock claiming to have freed Americans
jokes.punchline: Tracy calling out the historical hypocrisy of slavery-owning founding fathers
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary delivered through Tracy's character. Good historical awareness and righteous anger.

jokes.text: For a dude that has the most hilarious last name I ever heard, you blow.
jokes.setup: Tracy getting angry at John Hancock reenactor
jokes.punchline: Noting the irony that someone with 'Hancock' (hand-cock) as a name is disappointing
jokes.explanation: Good observation about the unintentionally funny name. Tracy's disappointment in the person matching the name is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: We didn't land on Plymouth Rock. Plymouth Rock landed on Mars!
jokes.setup: Tracy getting heated about historical inaccuracy
jokes.punchline: Garbling Malcolm X quote with complete nonsense about Mars
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly nonsensical misquoting of famous Malcolm X line. Perfect Tracy logic failure at the worst moment.

jokes.text: Because it's my birthday. And thanks for remembering. I'm 45. It's kind of a big deal.
jokes.setup: Writers asking why Lutz gets to pick lunch again
jokes.punchline: Lutz's pathetic birthday announcement that nobody remembered or cares about
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lutz patheticness. The defensive 'thanks for remembering' when nobody did is peak cringe.

jokes.text: Who orders food from a gas station? / The Shell by me has good hot dogs.
jokes.setup: Lutz's terrible restaurant choice from gas station
jokes.punchline: His defensive explanation about Shell having good hot dogs
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Lutz's terrible judgment. The specific Shell reference makes it more pathetic.

jokes.text: Dale Snitterman.
jokes.setup: Writers demanding to know who to blame if not Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately produces a specific scapegoat name
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the elaborate lie. The name 'Dale Snitterman' sounds perfectly like a hated corporate executive.

jokes.text: Snitterman is the one Who cancelled taco night in the commissary. Snitterman is the one who okayed that cast photo where your hair looks green.
jokes.setup: Liz establishing Dale Snitterman as the villain
jokes.punchline: Specific, petty grievances that everyone can relate to
jokes.explanation: Good specific grievances that feel real. Liz's quick improvisation of workplace complaints is impressive.

jokes.text: That vondruke!
jokes.setup: Everyone getting angry at Dale Snitterman
jokes.punchline: Jenna using her made-up German insult from earlier
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that maintains continuity. Shows Jenna sticking with her fake German persona.

jokes.text: The Salem witch trials, the red scare, global warming.
jokes.setup: Jack praising Liz's imaginary enemy strategy
jokes.punchline: Listing historical examples including controversial climate change reference
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary disguised as historical examples. The global warming inclusion is perfectly provocative for Jack's character.

jokes.text: Silas Marymount-Peppercorn
jokes.setup: Jack needing a fake name for house buying scheme
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously elaborate WASP-y fake name he invented
jokes.explanation: Perfectly over-the-top aristocratic name that sounds exactly like what Jack would invent. Great specificity.

jokes.text: The creative process was that I saw that name, forgot that I saw it, and said it later.
jokes.setup: Jack wondering about Liz's creative process with Dale Snitterman
jokes.punchline: Liz's honest confession about unconscious plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on creativity and unconscious influences. Perfect honesty about the writing process itself.

jokes.text: Then patriots are overrated. / Hey! What'd you say about the patriots? / I said they suck!
jokes.setup: Tracy denouncing patriots in Boston during historical tour
jokes.punchline: Locals thinking he's insulting their football team, leading to a fight
jokes.explanation: Perfect misunderstanding between historical and sports Patriots. Brilliant escalation that feels inevitable in Boston.

jokes.text: I am Silas Marymount-Peppercorn, And this is my first wife... Moronica.
jokes.setup: Kenneth playing Jack's fake identity to buy Nancy's house
jokes.punchline: The elaborate fake name plus calling Jenna 'first wife Moronica'
jokes.explanation: Great execution of the fake name with added insult to Jenna. 'First wife' implies polygamy, 'Moronica' is perfect.

jokes.text: My wife and I have disparate levels of attractiveness, because I am a successful inventor.
jokes.setup: Nancy wondering about the mismatched couple
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's matter-of-fact explanation of trophy wife dynamics
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent delivery of cynical relationship truth. 'Disparate levels of attractiveness' is perfectly clinical.

jokes.text: You little bitch. Look at you. You're just a suit. You're nobody!
jokes.setup: Writers confronting 'Dale Snitterman' (random office worker)
jokes.punchline: Frank's aggressive disappointment that their nemesis is just a regular guy
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the built-up anger. Frank's deflation at finding an ordinary person is realistic and funny.

jokes.text: Truffle shuffle! Truffle shuffle!
jokes.setup: Writers continuing to terrorize the innocent office worker
jokes.punchline: Making him do the humiliating Goonies dance
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference bullying. Works for the mob mentality but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Your neighbors named their daughter Belichick!
jokes.setup: Jack arguing Nancy shouldn't care what her neighbors think
jokes.punchline: The absurd example of naming a girl after the Patriots coach
jokes.explanation: Perfect specific example of Boston sports obsession taken too far. Great economy and regional accuracy.

jokes.text: let's get divorced. you marry the butler, And I'll be a gay octomom.
jokes.setup: Jack contrasting New York vs Boston attitudes toward change
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous example of New York relationship chaos
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd example of New York excess. 'Gay octomom' is a perfect specific nonsense phrase.

jokes.text: Really? So five years after Crispus Attucks was killed in the Boston massacre? / Run, Crispus. He... he's on to us!
jokes.setup: Tracy catching the historical reenactors in an inconsistency
jokes.punchline: The actors panicking and fleeing when caught in their historical error
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Tracy's historical knowledge versus the fake reenactors. The panic and flee response is brilliantly absurd.
